##NAMES=Gotzenite
##RRUFFID=R130110
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_4_NaCa_2_Ti(Si_2_O_7_)_2_(OF)F_2_
##LOCALITY=Pocos de Caldas, Pocos de Caldas Plateau, Minas Gerais, Brazil
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Gunnar Farber
##DESCRIPTION=Grey to pale brown aggregates of elongated crystals, associated with colorless nepheline, elongated pectolite, and white milky microcline.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130110
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Na_1.00_(Ca_4.23_Na_1.05_Zr_0.44_Mn_0.12_Sr_0.06_Ce_0.04_La_0.03_Fe_0.02_Y_0.02_Dy_0.01_)(Ti_0.90_Nd^5+^_0.10_)(Si_2.00_O_7_)_2_OF_2.98_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
